measured_evidence.interpretation: rewards far below -40 prove step-aggregate (multiple -40 drops summed); near-identical per-action means prove no learnable action gradient.
measured_evidence.after_interpretation: clean per-task rewards; local genuinely completes more (35% vs 14% vertical) -> real, state-dependent gradient now exists.

answers.reward_equation_matches_paper: yes (-Phi success / -C=40 drop / NaN no-arrival, Eq.20)
answers.reward_too_sparse_or_drop_dominated: drop penalty dominates magnitude, but the prior failure was credit mis-attribution, not sparsity.
answers.reward_varies_by_action: NO before fix (identical means); YES after fix.
answers.fixed_local_genuine_dominance: partly: local completes 35% vs vertical 14% under calibrated workload (workload bias), so local IS often best; but it should not be chosen unconditionally.
answers.state_features_predictive: rich 30-dim features incl per-action slack/feasibility/queue exist and vary.
answers.dueling_double_dqn_lstm: verified correct in prior repair; not modified.
answers.td_targets_stable: yes (rewards bounded to [-40,0] after fix; no -160/-174 spikes).
answers.learning_rate: 7e-7 paper value; small but exploration+credit-fix provide signal.
answers.replay_balanced: exploration restores action balance; no balancing sampling added.
answers.reward_normalization: raw reward kept; per-task credit (bounded) is the training target. No extra scaling applied.
answers.eval_collapse_cause: poorly separated Q from noisy reward (now addressed).
